response.justification: Bogdan brings strong distributed systems expertise from Dunnhumby and solid geospatial domain knowledge from GISCollective, plus demonstrated software craftsmanship with proven testing practices. However, his C++ experience is dated (2014–2015 at National Instruments), and the last six years have focused on TypeScript/Node.js, creating a significant language gap for a role where C++ is primary. More critically, there is no evidence of experience building navigation guidance systems or routing solutions, which are core responsibilities. While his ability to learn new technologies and his systems-thinking are assets, the combination of outdated primary language skills and missing domain expertise represents a substantial gap for a mid-senior position.
strengths: Strong distributed systems foundation from Dunnhumby (millions of req/sec) with proven ability to optimize high-throughput systems
Demonstrated geospatial domain knowledge through GISCollective open-source platform and Green Map navigation application work
Excellent software craftsmanship mindset with focus on testability, TDD, and quality (created Fluent-Asserts and Trial testing frameworks)
concerns: C++ expertise significantly outdated—last meaningful work in 2014–2015; primary recent focus is TypeScript/Node.js for 6+ years
No evidence of navigation guidance system development, routing algorithms, or traffic data integration experience required for core responsibilities
Career trajectory emphasizes web backends and full-stack development rather than systems-level C++ for data pipeline optimization

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and performance optimization skills
